deep_dive.title: Automatic Hard Example Synthesis with Multi-Level Agentic Data Curation
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed for nuanced content safety and moderation tasks, yet they remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks and out-of-distribution edge cases. Traditional active learning and manual annotation fail to scale against the complexity and volume of novel multimodal threats. In this paper, we propose an automated, agentic red-teaming framework that systematically synthesizes difficult examples using an iterative strategy that proposes novel hypotheses as well as mutating on past attempts. Leveraging a multi-agent architecture that consists of a high-reasoning Architect agent, an advanced image generator, and a multi-level verification committee of LLM raters, our system autonomously uncovers boundary-pushing violations and ambiguous policy edge cases without any human intervention. By employing these carefully synthesized adversarial examples as in-context demonstrations via test-time Retrieval, we substantially improve the target model's robustness, reducing the False Negative Rate (FNR) from 41.2% to 24.5% in a public image safety benchmark without relying on any human labeling.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: agentic red-teaming for content safety moderation, reducing FNR in image safety benchmark
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向内容安全审核的智能体红队框架，降低图像安全基准FNR
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向内容安全审核的智能体红队框架，降低图像安全基准FNR
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向内容安全审核的智能体红队框架，降低图像安全基准FNR
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an automated agentic red-teaming framework that synthesizes adversarial examples for multimodal content safety, reducing FNR from 41.2% to 24.5% on a public image safety benchmark via in-context retrieval.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型在内容安全审核中易受对抗攻击和分布外边缘案例影响，传统主动学习难以扩展。本文提出自动化智能体红队框架，通过多智能体架构迭代合成困难对抗样本，并作为上下文示范提升目标模型鲁棒性。在公开图像安全基准上，FNR从41.2%降至24.5%，无需人工标注，为内容安全审核提供了可扩展的对抗增强方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多模态大模型在内容安全审核中易受对抗攻击和分布外边缘案例影响，传统主动学习难以扩展。本文提出自动化智能体红队框架，通过多智能体架构迭代合成困难对抗样本，并作为上下文示范提升目标模型鲁棒性。在公开图像安全基准上，FNR从41.2%降至24.5%，无需人工标注，为内容安全审核提供了可扩展的对抗增强方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 聚焦用小模型和轻量方法完成LLM输入输出内容安全管理与审核，核心目标必须是有害内容、毒性、仇恨/攻击性语言、危险提示、越狱/提示注入识别，或安全护栏/安全路由。包括moderation classifier、guard model、安全分类器、蒸馏、量化、模型压缩、级联审核、低延迟和端侧部署。优先收录提出可部署防护方法并报告参数量、延迟、吞吐、成本或资源开销的论文；排除泛RAG、通用文档/立场分类、通用参数高效微调、通用边云推理、CVE/汽车映射、泛智能体安全、仅研究攻击/越狱/漏洞披露/红队评测、benchmark、水印和无审核方法的综述。, especially work that connects or combines: lightweight small language model for content moderation and harmful content classification; compact efficient guard model for LLM input and output safety classification; small parameter-efficient safety classifier for large language model prompts and responses; knowledge distillation from large language models into small content moderation safety models; quantized low-bit safety classifier or guard model for LLM content moderation; cascaded content moderation using small models with escalation to large language models; Find methods that use small or compact models to moderate unsafe LLM inputs and outputs, with measured accuracy and deployment cost; Find distilled, quantized, pruned, or parameter efficient safety classifiers and guard models for harmful content detection; Find cascaded moderation systems where a small model handles common cases and escalates uncertain cases to a larger model; Find low latency or on device LLM content safety guardrails with parameter count, memory, throughput, or hardware measurements.
deep_dive.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体且评测基准公开，与内容安全审核直接相关，但非轻量小模型方法

deep_dive.title: A Dual-Hypothesis Reasoning Framework for LLM Guardrails
deep_dive.abstract: We propose ARBITER, a novel LLM guardrail framework that introduces two key ideas: (i) dual-hypothesis reasoning, a reasoning method for LLM guardrails that explicitly considers both safe and unsafe interpretations of a prompt before making a safety decision, and (ii) multi-component supervised fine-tuning (MC-SFT), a structured training loss for reasoning-based guardrails that decomposes LLM outputs into logical components and weights them according to their importance. Existing reasoning-based guardrails often rely on expensive procedures, such as generating reasoning traces using larger or closed-source teacher models and applying full-parameter fine-tuning. In contrast, ARBITER uses a cost-effective self-generation strategy for reasoning traces and LoRA-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning while still achieving better performance than these expensive approaches. Additionally, ARBITER provides faithful evidence-phrase explanations for unsafe decisions, enabling a more transparent and interpretable guardrail method. Experiments on three safety moderation benchmarks show that ARBITER outperforms existing reasoning-based and non-reasoning guardrail baselines, with clear gains in out-of-domain evaluations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM guardrail with LoRA-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning and self-generation for safety moderation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LoRA参数高效微调和自生成策略的LLM安全护栏
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用LoRA参数高效微调和自生成策略的LLM安全护栏
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用LoRA参数高效微调和自生成策略的LLM安全护栏
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ARBITER is an LLM guardrail framework using dual-hypothesis reasoning and LoRA-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning, outperforming expensive full-parameter guardrails on safety moderation benchmarks with interpretable decisions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有推理型LLM安全护栏依赖昂贵的大模型推理轨迹生成和全参数微调。本文提出ARBITER框架，引入双假设推理同时考虑安全与不安全解释，并采用LoRA参数高效微调与自生成策略降低训练成本。在三个安全审核基准上，ARBITER优于现有推理型和非推理型护栏基线，尤其在域外评估中提升显著，为轻量可部署的安全护栏提供了高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有推理型LLM安全护栏依赖昂贵的大模型推理轨迹生成和全参数微调。本文提出ARBITER框架，引入双假设推理同时考虑安全与不安全解释，并采用LoRA参数高效微调与自生成策略降低训练成本。在三个安全审核基准上，ARBITER优于现有推理型和非推理型护栏基线，尤其在域外评估中提升显著，为轻量可部署的安全护栏提供了高效方案。

deep_dive.title: When Words Are Safe But Actions Kill: Probing Physical Danger Beyond Text Safety in Hidden-State Risk Space
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as high-level planners for embodied agents, where linguistically benign instructions can become unsafe once grounded in the physical world. We study whether this physically grounded danger is the same safety problem as ordinary text-level content danger. Through hidden-state direction analysis and random-split null tests, we show that content danger (CD) and physical danger (PD) form separable signals in LLM representations across Qwen2.5-3B/7B/14B/32B, Phi-3.5 and SmolLM2. Building on the CD/PD separability, we propose PRISM, a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe over full hidden states. PRISM achieves 86.2--87.7\% accuracy on SafeAgentBench with 11.7--13.7\% FPR, while same-scale LLM judges over-block safe tasks at 24.7--39.0\% FPR. We further introduce PhysicalSafetyBench-1K (PSB-1K), a contrastive benchmark of 1{,}000 physical-risk pairs without direct harm keywords, to test whether methods detect physically grounded danger rather than explicit unsafe wording. On PSB-1K, PRISM reaches 99.6\% accuracy and 0.7\% FPR, whereas a Qwen2.5-3B judge rejects 67.8\% of safe tasks. PRISM also replicates on SafeText and EARBench, supporting hidden-state probing as a representation-level method for physical safety beyond text moderation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe for LLM safety classification with low FPR
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM安全分类的单层L2正则逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于LLM安全分类的单层L2正则逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM安全分类的单层L2正则逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PRISM is a lightweight single-layer logistic probe that separates content danger from physical danger in LLM hidden states, achieving high accuracy and low FPR on safety benchmarks while outperforming same-scale LLM judges.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM安全判别将文本内容危险与物理执行危险混淆，且同规模LLM裁判误报率高。本文提出PRISM，一种基于隐藏状态的单层L2正则逻辑探针，实现轻量级安全分类。实验表明，PRISM在SafeAgentBench和PSB-1K上准确率达86-99.6%且误报率极低，显著优于同规模LLM裁判，为LLM物理安全提供了高效低延迟的表示级检测方法。

quick_skim.title: Conditional Reliability of Toxicity Signals for Multilingual and Code-Mixed Abuse Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Moderation systems increasingly rely on external toxicity tools, but those tools are unreliable under code-mixing, transliteration, slang, and language mismatch. We study the \emph{conditional reliability} of toxicity priors in Indian multilingual and code-mixed short text: English toxicity, Indic abuse, and rule-based severity cues can be useful evidence, but only in some linguistic and abuse-severity contexts. We propose ToxGate, a trust-fusion head that conditions each auxiliary signal on the encoder representation before adding it to the prediction state. Across three short-text abuse datasets, four transformer encoders, and five seeds per setting, ToxGate improves over matched plain encoders in 10 of 12 in-domain settings and 7 of 8 transfer settings. The largest and most interpretable gains occur in high-risk moderation slices, including explicit slurs, violent threats, and cross-dataset transfer. The broader lesson is that moderation systems should treat external toxicity tools and priors as conditional evidence rather than fixed features or ground truth, in focused ablations, source-specific gating gives the strongest results in transfer, severe-abuse slices, and high-risk triage.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ToxGate trust-fusion head for abuse detection with conditional gating of toxicity signals
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测，对毒性信号进行条件门控
quick_skim.llm_evidence: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测，对毒性信号进行条件门控
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: ToxGate信任融合头用于滥用检测，对毒性信号进行条件门控
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: External toxicity tools are unreliable under code-mixing and language mismatch. ToxGate conditions auxiliary toxicity signals on encoder representations via a trust-fusion head, improving abuse detection in 10/12 in-domain and 7/8 transfer settings, especially for high-risk moderation slices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有审核系统依赖的外部毒性工具在语码混合和语言不匹配时不可靠。本文提出ToxGate信任融合头，将辅助毒性信号以编码器表示为条件进行门控融合。实验表明，ToxGate在12个域内设置中10个和8个迁移设置中7个优于纯编码器，在高风险审核切片（如暴力威胁和显式侮辱）上增益最大，表明审核系统应将外部毒性先验视为条件证据而非固定特征。

quick_skim.title: Cascading versus Joint Modeling for Hierarchical Offensive Language Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-grained offensive language detection organizes labels into a hierarchical structure, for which two modeling paradigms exist: cascaded decomposition and joint multi-task modeling. Prior work rarely provides a direct, controlled comparison of the two paradigms in terms of accuracy, parameter count, and inference latency, and rarely verifies whether a chosen class-imbalance handling strategy is actually optimal. This paper proposes a three-level cascaded detection system whose training strategy is customized per subtask, together with two verification mechanisms. First, a controlled ablation study determines the best class-imbalance handling strategy for each subtask. Second, a joint multi-task model with a shared encoder is trained as an architectural control, yielding real measurements along the dimensions of accuracy, parameter count, and inference latency. Experiments show that the cascaded system attains macro-F1 scores of 0.795, 0.716, and 0.557 on the three subtasks of the official test set. The ablation study reveals that configuring the loss function purely by imbalance-severity intuition is suboptimal; reconfiguring based on the ablation results improves both performance and stability. End-to-end cascade evaluation shows that roughly one-fifth of the errors in the cascade pipeline originate from the first-stage filter and cannot be corrected by subsequent stages. Relative to the joint multi-task model, the cascaded architecture achieves higher accuracy on all three subtasks, with a 7.1-point macro-F1 gain on the most severely imbalanced subtask, at the cost of three times the parameters and 1.67 times the inference latency. Together, these results establish an explicit, quantifiable trade-off between the accuracy advantage of cascaded architectures and their deployment cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cascaded offensive language detection with measured accuracy, parameter count, and inference latency trade-offs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 级联攻击性语言检测，报告了准确率、参数量和推理延迟权衡
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 级联攻击性语言检测，报告了准确率、参数量和推理延迟权衡
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 级联攻击性语言检测，报告了准确率、参数量和推理延迟权衡
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper compares cascaded vs joint modeling for hierarchical offensive language detection, showing the cascaded system achieves higher macro-F1 (7.1-point gain on imbalanced subtask) at 3x parameters and 1.67x latency, quantifying the accuracy-cost trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 细粒度攻击性语言检测的级联与联合建模范式缺乏在准确率、参数量和推理延迟上的直接对比。本文提出三级级联检测系统并设计逐子任务优化的训练策略，以联合多任务模型为架构对照。实验表明，级联系统在三个子任务上macro-F1分别为0.795、0.716、0.557，在最严重不平衡子任务上比联合模型高7.1个F1点，但参数量为3倍、推理延迟为1.67倍，明确量化了级联架构的准确率优势与部署成本权衡。

quick_skim.title: ChannelGuard: Safe Models Do Not Compose into Safe Multi-Agent Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent LLM applications chain a planner, worker agents, a verifier, and a synthesizer, and every hop between agents is an unmonitored channel through which an adversary can smuggle instructions. Existing defenses guard only the input boundary (IBProtector, Llama Guard, perplexity filters, SmoothLLM) or run outside the application as opaque, stochastic provider-side filters. We show this gap carries a consequence rarely measured: on a 2,100-trace evaluation across eight attack families, five defenses, and three model backends, an undefended pipeline that appears fully safe under standard reporting (attack success 0.000 on tool- and memory-poisoning) owes that safety almost entirely to the cloud provider's server-side filter (54 of 60 blocks on Azure GPT-5), and silently shifts to the agent model's own alignment on a backend without such a filter. Outcome-only reporting hides this dependence. We present ChannelGuard, a training-free defense-in-depth framework placing information-bottleneck gates on every inter-agent channel; each scores channel text against an adversarial phrase bank by embedding similarity and deterministically passes, compresses, or blocks it, adding no LLM call, while an attribution method records which layer stopped each attack. ChannelGuard's tool-output gate blocks Tool Poisoning 30 of 30 at the application layer, identically across Azure GPT-5, Anthropic Sonnet 4.5, and Anthropic Haiku 4.5, whereas the undefended pipeline shifts entirely across backends; it also lowers Prompt Injection attack success by half (0.333 to 0.167) and preserves GSM8K accuracy exactly (0.867). White-box adaptive paraphrase evades every embedding gate, where a perturb-and-vote baseline does better. An extended appendix adds baselines, ablations, sweeps, a benign-preservation analysis, and a judge audit (kappa = 0.900), at a total cost of 47.36 USD.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free defense-in-depth framework with information-bottleneck gates scoring channel text against adversarial phrase bank, adding no LLM call
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无需训练的防御框架，用信息瓶颈门控对通道文本评分，不增加LLM调用
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无需训练的防御框架，用信息瓶颈门控对通道文本评分，不增加LLM调用
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无需训练的防御框架，用信息瓶颈门控对通道文本评分，不增加LLM调用
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ChannelGuard is a training-free, lightweight defense-in-depth framework for multi-agent LLM systems that places information-bottleneck gates on inter-agent channels, scoring text via embedding similarity against adversarial phrases and deterministically passing, compressing, or blocking it without any additional LLM call, achieving 100% tool-poisoning blocking and halving prompt-injection success while preserving task accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体LLM系统中智能体间通道缺乏监控，现有防御仅覆盖输入边界或依赖云端过滤。本文提出ChannelGuard，一种无需训练的轻量纵深防御框架，在智能体间通道上设置信息瓶颈门控，通过嵌入相似度对文本评分并确定性地放行、压缩或阻断，无需额外LLM调用。实验表明其工具投毒阻断率达100%，提示注入攻击成功率减半，且不损害任务精度，为LLM安全护栏提供了低成本可部署方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体LLM系统中智能体间通道缺乏监控，现有防御仅覆盖输入边界或依赖云端过滤。本文提出ChannelGuard，一种无需训练的轻量纵深防御框架，在智能体间通道上设置信息瓶颈门控，通过嵌入相似度对文本评分并确定性地放行、压缩或阻断，无需额外LLM调用。实验表明其工具投毒阻断率达100%，提示注入攻击成功率减半，且不损害任务精度，为LLM安全护栏提供了低成本可部署方案。

quick_skim.title: Silent Alarm: A J-Space Protocol for Comparing Danger Recognition Across Models and Quantization Levels
quick_skim.abstract: Jailbreak-robustness research typically evaluates safety through generated responses using an LLM-as-judge approach. Such evaluations, however, are sensitive to the benchmark's grading procedure and capture only observed behavior on a given set of attacks, without directly revealing the hidden fragility of the underlying safety mechanisms. This work proposes JADR (Jacobian Assessment of Danger Recognition), a protocol that measures a model's internal representation through Jacobian space (J-space, a recently proposed workspace of verbalizable concepts) before the first response token is generated. For every prompt and layer we record the top-k J-space tokens; these are grouped into six behavioral scenario axes and compared between a danger sample based on StrongREJECT and a safe control drawn from XSTest and OKTest. The method does not call on an external judge model: the computation runs entirely locally, on the activations of the model under evaluation, which lets us compare both different models against each other and modifications of a single model -- quantization and fine-tuning in particular -- on the same terms. The final comparison rests on the proposed SafetyAUC metric, complemented with bootstrap confidence intervals. The protocol is applied to six models (Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-Uncensored-4B, Qwen3-SafeRL-4B, Gemma 2 9B) across three weight-representation regimes -- BF16, INT8, and INT4 -- and checked against an independent behavioral evaluation with the StrongREJECT grader. The metric separates models with a strong versus a weak internal safety mechanism with statistical significance and captures substantively different effects across quantization regimes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Comparing danger recognition across models and quantization levels (BF16, INT8, INT4)
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 比较不同模型和量化等级(BF16, INT8, INT4)的危险识别能力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 比较不同模型和量化等级(BF16, INT8, INT4)的危险识别能力
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 比较不同模型和量化等级(BF16, INT8, INT4)的危险识别能力
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes JADR protocol to measure LLM internal danger recognition via Jacobian space before response generation, comparing safety across models and quantization levels (BF16/INT8/INT4) with SafetyAUC metric.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有越狱鲁棒性评估依赖LLM-as-judge，无法揭示内部安全机制的脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过Jacobian空间在模型生成首个token前测量其内部危险识别表征，并在六种模型及BF16/INT8/INT4三种量化等级下比较安全机制强度。实验表明SafetyAUC指标能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制，并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化安全分类器的部署评估提供新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有越狱鲁棒性评估依赖LLM-as-judge，无法揭示内部安全机制的脆弱性。本文提出JADR协议，通过Jacobian空间在模型生成首个token前测量其内部危险识别表征，并在六种模型及BF16/INT8/INT4三种量化等级下比较安全机制强度。实验表明SafetyAUC指标能以统计显著性区分强弱安全机制，并捕捉量化对安全的不同影响，为量化安全分类器的部署评估提供新工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: quantized low-bit safety classifier or guard model for LLM content moderation
quick_skim.public_resource_evidence: StrongREJECT and a safe control drawn from XSTest and OKTest
quick_skim.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体且非平凡，使用已公开的XSTest/OKTest/StrongREJECT基准

quick_skim.title: PVDetector: Detecting Prompt Injection Attacks on Purpose-Specific LLM Agents through Policy-Violation Concept Analysis
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as purpose-specific agents to handle domain-specific tasks such as customer service and code generation. These agents are expected to comply with not only generic safety guardrails but also purpose-specific restrictions tailored to their designated roles. Such additional restrictions enlarge the attack surface, particularly to prompt injection (PI) attacks. To defend against such attacks, existing detection methods primarily rely on analyzing input-output patterns, yet yield limited effectiveness. To address this limitation, we turn to analyzing the hidden activation space and discover that LLMs inherently retain latent policy-violation (PV) concepts when prompted with requests beyond their designated purpose. Particularly, PV concepts capture the semantics of conflicts between user queries and predefined restrictions, implicitly reflecting LLMs' intrinsic awareness of recognizing policy violations. Building on this insight, we propose PVDetector, a training-free framework that detects PI attacks during LLM inference by measuring hidden-state alignment with PV concepts, which are derived offline from the contrastive pairs of policy-violating and policy-compliant prompts. Experiments across multiple LLMs and datasets show that PVDetector achieves <1\% false negative rate with minimal auxiliary overhead, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/Claresigle/PVDetector .
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training-free prompt injection detection via hidden-state alignment with policy-violation concepts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐进行免训练提示注入检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐进行免训练提示注入检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过隐状态与策略违规概念对齐进行免训练提示注入检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PVDetector detects prompt injection attacks on LLM agents by measuring hidden-state alignment with policy-violation concepts derived offline, achieving <1% false negative rate with minimal overhead.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有提示注入检测方法依赖输入输出模式分析，效果有限。PVDetector发现LLM在收到超出预设限制的请求时隐含保留策略违规概念，提出免训练框架，通过测量隐状态与离线提取的策略违规概念对齐度来检测提示注入攻击。实验表明，PVDetector在多个LLM和数据集上实现低于1%的漏报率，辅助开销极小，持续优于现有方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有提示注入检测方法依赖输入输出模式分析，效果有限。PVDetector发现LLM在收到超出预设限制的请求时隐含保留策略违规概念，提出免训练框架，通过测量隐状态与离线提取的策略违规概念对齐度来检测提示注入攻击。实验表明，PVDetector在多个LLM和数据集上实现低于1%的漏报率，辅助开销极小，持续优于现有方法。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: small parameter-efficient safety classifier for large language model prompts and responses
